forecast-period.text: A high pressure system centred over Eyre Peninsula will move slowly eastwards this week and a couple of cold fronts will move across the far southeast of SA on Tuesday and Thursday. A second high pressure system is expected to follow and will become established south of the Bight late Thursday then will move eastwards during Friday and Saturday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area, contracting to near southern coasts in the evening. Sunny in the north. Maximum temperatures below average. Mostly light to moderate southwest to southeasterly winds. Afternoon sea breezes near the coast.

Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area, mostly about southern coastal areas, contracting to southern coasts later. Dry elsewhere. Southwest to southeasterly winds tending easterly in the north. Afternoon sea breezes near the coast.

Morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Showers about the Lower South East, and isolated showers possible about remaining southern coasts. Sunny in the north. Maximum temperatures slightly above average in the far south west but near or slightly below average elsewhere. Mostly light northeast to northwesterly winds. Afternoon sea breezes near the coast.

text.p: Isolated showers about southern coastal districts, more frequent about the Lower South East district. Mostly sunny in the north. Maximum temperatures near average. Northwesterly winds in the south, freshening near southern coats, ahead of a southwesterly change in the afternoon. Generally light winds in the north.

Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area, clearing during the evening. Maximum temperatures below average about the agricultural area but near or slightly above average elsewhere. South to southeasterly winds, tending southeast to northeasterly in the west.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Friday: less than 2 mm about the southern agricultural area, increasing to local falls of 2 to 5 mm about southern coasts and ranges and the Lower South East district. Isolated falls of 5 to 10 mm are possible about the Lower South East.
